meta.name: The Wandering Court
meta.description: A royal court in exile travels the realm; you are its Seneschal, summoning champions and settling its intrigues chapter by chapter.
meta.author: axm-arc team

attributes.name: Force
attributes.description: Battle power — the strength that wins a bout, breaks a line, or ends an argument the hard way.

attributes.name: Artifice
attributes.description: Craft and subtlety — the cunning that binds a pact, reads a poisoned dish, or turns a rival's own trick against them.

attributes.name: Grace
attributes.description: Poise and speed — the composure that holds a crowd's eye and the quickness that gets there first.

attributes.name: Resolve
attributes.description: Will and spirit — the steadiness that keeps a retinue together when the court itself has none to spare.

progressionTiers.name: Interlude I — The Road
progressionTiers.flavorText: The exiled court's first season is spent just surviving the road: a bandit toll, a village's old grievance, a river that wants a pact before it grants passage.

progressionTiers.name: Interlude II — The Rival Court
progressionTiers.flavorText: A rival court takes notice of the wanderers and starts testing them properly: a masked envoy, a tourney of claims, a banquet with poison under the courses.

progressionTiers.name: Finale — The Eclipsed Throne
progressionTiers.flavorText: The rival court's own seat stands between the Seneschal and the end of the exile: a silent procession first, and then the eclipsed throne itself.

challenges.name: The Bandit Toll
challenges.description: On the road into exile, a bandit crew has set up a toll no royal seal can excuse. The court can spare little blood and less coin, and the bandits know it.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Line of Honor
mechanicChecks.description: The retinue holds formation so the bandits can't pick off the baggage train.

mechanicChecks.name: Read the Room
mechanicChecks.description: Every retainer sizes up the toll captain's mood before anyone speaks first.

success.narrative: The bandit captain takes one look at the retinue's line and decides the toll isn't worth collecting today. The road south lies open.

partial.narrative: The toll is broken, but the captain slips off to warn the next crew down the road. The court moves on regardless.

failure.narrative: The bandits hold the toll longer than expected. The court falls back to regroup a mile short of the crossing.

challenges.name: The Village Grievance
challenges.description: A village along the road has an old grievance against the last court that passed through, and won't let this one pass until it's heard and settled.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Standard
mechanicChecks.description: The Herald keeps the court's banner steady through an airing of decades-old grievances.

mechanicChecks.name: Settle the Ledger
mechanicChecks.description: The retinue works through the village's account of wrongs without reopening old wounds.

success.narrative: The grievance is heard, answered, and finally laid to rest. The village ties its ribbon to the court's banner as the retinue moves on.

partial.narrative: The grievance is settled, but not to everyone's satisfaction. The village lets the court pass anyway.

failure.narrative: The hearing collapses into old arguments no one present can settle. The village turns the court away for the season.

challenges.name: The River-Crossing Pact
challenges.description: The river ahead won't be forded on nerve alone — it wants a pact, properly bound, before it grants safe passage to a court it doesn't recognize.

mechanicChecks.name: Bind the Pact
mechanicChecks.description: The Arcanist inscribes the crossing-pact so the river's spirits honor the court's passage.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Line of Honor
mechanicChecks.description: The retinue holds the crossing formation while the pact takes hold beneath them.

mechanicChecks.name: Read the Room
mechanicChecks.description: Every retainer watches the riverbank for spirits that never agreed to any pact.

success.narrative: The pact holds and the crossing is granted. The river will remember this court, for once, kindly.

partial.narrative: The pact binds just long enough. Half the baggage train gets wet, but the court crosses.

failure.narrative: The pact fails to take, and the river refuses passage outright. The court camps another cold night on the near bank.

challenges.name: The Masked Envoy
challenges.description: The rival court sends a masked envoy to test the wanderers with claims no one can verify and tricks no one can call out — not without proof, and not without a mask to unmask first.

mechanicChecks.name: Unmask the Envoy
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion corners the rival court's masked envoy before the tricks land.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Standard
mechanicChecks.description: The Herald holds the court's composure so the envoy can't read a bluff in it.

mechanicChecks.name: Cut the False Claims
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion's retinue seals off every exit the envoy's masked allies might use to slip false claims past the court.

success.narrative: The mask comes off in front of both retinues, and the envoy's claims come apart with it. The rival court will test more carefully next time.

partial.narrative: The mask cracks but the envoy is gone before it falls all the way off. The claims are discredited regardless.

failure.narrative: The envoy's claims stick, unanswered. The rival court takes it as a concession.

challenges.name: The Tourney of Claims
challenges.description: The rival court settles disputed precedence the old way: a tourney, fought in front of both retinues, with the loser's claims forfeit for a generation.

mechanicChecks.name: Win the Tourney Bout
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion fights the rival court's claimant in the tourney lists, in full view of both courts.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Line of Honor
mechanicChecks.description: The retinue holds its ground in the tourney stands, roped off from the rival court's partisans.

success.narrative: The Champion takes the bout clean, and the rival claimant's precedence goes with it. The tourney stands go quiet, then loud.

partial.narrative: The bout is won on a technicality neither court fully agrees on. The claim is forfeit anyway.

failure.narrative: The bout goes to the rival claimant. The wandering court's precedence takes a public hit.

challenges.name: The Poisoned Banquet
challenges.description: The rival court hosts a banquet meant to humiliate the wanderers quietly — and, if the Seneschal isn't careful, permanently. Someone has to taste every course before anyone else does.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Line of Honor
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion's retinue holds the banquet hall's doors against the rival court's armed footmen.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Standard
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion keeps the retinue together through a banquet staged to humiliate the court course by course.

mechanicChecks.name: Detect the Poison
mechanicChecks.description: The Arcanist reads the banquet's dishes for the rival court's favored toxins before anyone lifts a cup.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Sick
mechanicChecks.description: The Confessor tends whoever drinks before the poison is caught.

success.narrative: Every dish is caught before it's swallowed, and the humiliation the rival court planned lands on no one. The banquet ends in the wanderers' favor.

partial.narrative: The poison is caught, but not before someone feels its edge. The banquet ends without a scandal, at a cost.

failure.narrative: The banquet's tricks land as planned. The wandering court leaves the hall humiliated in front of the realm.

challenges.name: The Silent Procession
challenges.description: The rival court's household guard seals the ceremonial procession route in a ritual hush that hasn't been broken in a generation. Every step through it is watched, and no one is supposed to speak.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Line of Honor
mechanicChecks.description: The retinue forces passage through the procession route the rival court has sealed with its own household guard.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Standard
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion keeps the procession's shape as the crowd presses in from both sides.

mechanicChecks.name: Read the Veil-Signs
mechanicChecks.description: The Herald traces the veiled signals the rival court is passing through the silent crowd, with the Arcanist's craft behind the reading.

mechanicChecks.name: Read the Room
mechanicChecks.description: Every retainer keeps a still face while the procession's ritual silence turns openly hostile.

success.narrative: The silence breaks for the first time in a generation, and the procession's veiled signals are read in full. The rival court's seat has no ceremony left to hide behind.

partial.narrative: The procession is forced open, but the household guard's captain slips out with word of it. The route is passable regardless.

failure.narrative: The ritual hush holds. The retinue is turned back from the procession route, the silence unbroken.

challenges.name: The Eclipsed Throne
challenges.description: The rival court's own seat, and the throne it eclipses every other claim in the realm from. It has nowhere left to retreat to, and neither does the wandering court — this is where the exile is decided, one way or the other.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Line of Honor
mechanicChecks.description: The last hall in the rival court's seat, and its household guard has been told to give no ground.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Standard
mechanicChecks.description: The Champion carries the retinue through the throne hall against the rival court's own champions.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Sick
mechanicChecks.description: The Confessor keeps the retinue standing through a fight with no ground to give and no aid coming.

mechanicChecks.name: Read the Room
mechanicChecks.description: Every retainer reads the eclipsed throne hall for the rival court's last tricks before the final push.

success.narrative: The eclipsed throne falls, and with it the exile. The wandering court is wandering no longer — it made sure of that, chapter by chapter.

partial.narrative: The throne hall falls, but the rival court's claimant is gone by the time the retinue reaches it. The seat is the wanderers'; the claimant is someone else's problem now.

failure.narrative: The household guard holds the throne hall. The retinue withdraws from the seat, the exile not yet ended.

items.name: Toll-Keeper's Coin
items.flavorText: A coin cut from a bandit toll that no longer applies to this court.

items.name: Grievance Ribbon
items.flavorText: Faded ribbon a village elder pinned to the court's banner rather than let the grievance go unmarked.

items.name: Pact-Sealed Oar
items.flavorText: Carved by the Arcanist's own hand, still humming faintly with the river's bargain.

items.name: Envoy's Cracked Mask
items.flavorText: Its enamel is fractured down the left eye, right where the Champion's gauntlet caught it.

items.name: Tourney Champion's Favor
items.flavorText: A rival court's favor, won honestly in front of both courts and worn ever since.

items.name: Banquet Antidote Phial
items.flavorText: Half-empty; the Confessor insists on keeping it that way.

items.name: Procession Standard-Veil
items.flavorText: A veil torn from the rival court's own standard during the silent procession's one loud moment.

items.name: Eclipsed Crown
items.flavorText: Plain silver eclipsed by no gem — the only crown the wandering court ever needed back.

narrativeEvents.title: The First Season on the Road
narrativeEvents.text: The toll falls without a single royal seal spent. The wandering court's first season away from its throne begins in earnest, off the map and out of anyone's ledger.

narrativeEvents.title: The Road Season Ends
narrativeEvents.text: The road's bandits, grievances, and river spirits are all settled. The court has proven it can survive its own exile before it ever tries to reclaim anything.

narrativeEvents.title: The Rival Season Turns
narrativeEvents.text: Envoy, tourney, and banquet — the rival court's every gambit spent and answered. Word of the wandering court's Seneschal starts to travel ahead of the retinue itself.

narrativeEvents.title: The Silence Breaks
narrativeEvents.text: The procession's hush cracks for the first time in a generation. The rival court's seat has no ceremony left to hide behind.

narrativeEvents.title: The Court Comes Home
narrativeEvents.text: The eclipsed throne falls, and the wandering court is wandering no longer. Every champion summoned, every chapter written on the road, ends here — in a season that finally holds.
